summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the 1988 Chicago show with a stellar Scarlet‑Fire opener, soaring The Other One and crisp drums‑to‑Space, despite a few mixed moments.
review: The Miller soundboard source delivers crystal‑clear audio that lets listeners hear every nuance of this 1988 Rosemont performance. Highlights include an outer‑space‑level Scarlet>Fire opener, a powerhouse second set driven by The Other One and an inspired drums‑to‑Space segment, while a few reviewers note a rough Louie Louie jam and the inevitable wear in Jerry’s voice.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.56/5; most comments praise both the musical performance and the high‑quality soundboard recording, with only occasional minor criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller collection soundboard is described as crisp, clear and well‑balanced; reviewers note great room ambience and no significant dropouts or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A thrilling Scarlet>Fire opener sets an outer‑space tone, the first set stays solid, and the second set builds high energy with a tight drums‑to‑Space segment and a memorable The Other One, though a few covers (e.g., Louie Louie) feel uneven.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work is praised as powerful and determined; some note his voice sounds aged but still expressive.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum/space segment is highlighted as inspired and a high point of the performance.
